scope: Complete agent-readable AetherEdge repository catalog. The public documentation site applies a separate publication allowlist.

documents.title: AetherEdge Agent Instructions
documents.description: This file is the canonical instruction source for coding agents working in this repository. CLAUDE.md and GEMINI.md are symlinks to it, so every agent reads and edits the same content. Any further tool-specific file may add usage notes,…

documents.title: Aether Architecture
documents.description: Aether is migrating from a Redis-centred multi-service EMS product to an AI-native, industry-neutral edge kernel. The target architecture and migration rules are defined in:

documents.title: AetherEdge
documents.description: Repository overview, current product status, installation paths, architecture boundaries, and development entry points.

documents.description: Machine-readable AetherEdge catalog.yaml resource.

documents.description: AI evaluations are scenario fixtures that verify capability discovery, authorization, confirmation, audit, and failure behavior. They complement Rust unit tests; they do not replace deterministic runtime tests.

documents.title: Machine-readable contracts
documents.description: This directory is reserved for stable configuration, command, event, and MCP schemas. Generated files must carry a generated header and are updated through the repository generation task rather than edited manually.

documents.title: CloudLink v1 alpha 1
documents.description: AetherContracts is the sole interoperability authority for this protocol slice. Product-repository wire profiles, manifests, gates, and evidence files are non-authoritative implementation overlays and may not add or redefine wire fields.…

documents.title: Consumer distribution v1 alpha 1
documents.description: This profile lets AetherCloud, AetherIot, and independent implementations pin one exact AetherContracts release while keeping their default verification path offline. Distribution conformance proves release identity and byte integrity; it…

documents.title: Language-neutral TCK v1 alpha 1
documents.description: Repository tests compile every normative JSON Schema, validate valid and wire-invalid fixtures, preserve context-invalid distinctions, and verify every declared SHA-256.

documents.title: Experimental CloudLink contracts
documents.description: v1/ is AetherEdge's integration surface for the public AetherContracts release. AetherContracts v0.1.0-alpha.3, pinned by the root consumer lock, is shared authority. The local files are adopted byte copies, migration history, or product…

documents.title: Candidate reconciliation and remaining work
documents.description: Public AetherContracts alpha.3 replaces the incompatible repository-local experimental vocabularies previously called the AetherEdge candidate and 1.0-cloud.1. The public contract selects the AetherEdge durable delivery, digest, cursor,…

documents.title: CloudLink MQTT v1 AetherEdge implementation overlay
documents.description: This directory consumes AetherContracts v0.1.0-alpha.3, the sole CloudLink wire authority. Imported files are exact release bytes. Local manifests, profiles, gates, scenarios, and migration notes are non-authoritative AetherEdge…

documents.title: Aether Data Processing JSON Schemas
documents.description: This directory publishes the Draft 2020-12 transport schemas for version 1 of the Aether Data Processing contract family.

documents.title: Core Crate Instructions
documents.description: These instructions apply to all crates in this directory.

documents.title: aether-application
documents.description: Transport-neutral command and query use cases for the Aether edge kernel.

documents.title: aether-cloudlink
documents.description: Transport-neutral implementation of the experimental, digest-pinned public AetherContracts CloudLink subset. It provides strict closed JSON decoding, RFC 8785 business digests, session/version/epoch validation, stable delivery envelopes,…

documents.title: aether-data-processing crate
documents.description: Strict, transport-neutral JSON codec for the Aether Data Processing v1 processor boundary. It converts validated domain values to and from the versioned RFC 3339/JSON DTOs, encodes Aether-accepted DerivedData, and computes RFC 8785 input…

documents.title: aether-dataplane
documents.description: aether-dataplane is Aether's business-neutral shared-memory core. It can be used by an embedded gateway without Redis, PostgreSQL, SQLx, routing models, or any HTTP stack.

documents.title: aether-domain
documents.description: Industry-neutral, nostd domain types for the Aether edge kernel.

documents.title: aether-integration-contract
documents.description: Strict product-side Rust binding for the experimental AetherContracts aether.integration v1alpha1 contract in the 0.1.0-alpha.4 candidate.

documents.title: aether-integration-control
documents.description: Experimental, default-off AetherEdge binding for the frozen AetherContracts integration-control v1alpha1 candidate in release 0.1.0-alpha.4.

documents.title: aether-pack
documents.description: Versioned, industry-neutral domain-pack manifest loading for the Aether edge kernel. The crate validates a pack's own release identity, distribution identity, compatible Aether range, required capability and protocol IDs, explicitly…

documents.title: aether-ports
documents.description: Small, object-safe capability interfaces for Aether edge extensions.

documents.title: aether-edge-sdk
documents.description: Versioned beta facade for embedding the Aether AI-native IoT edge kernel.

documents.title: aether-testkit
documents.description: Reusable conformance checks and deterministic test doubles for Aether extension authors.

documents.title: Legacy CLI guide
documents.description: Migration-period CLI guide retained for historical lookup; the current CLI reference remains authoritative.

documents.description: Compatibility entry that routes readers to service-owned OpenAPI instead of duplicating endpoint definitions.

documents.title: Legacy AetherEMS development quickstart

documents.title: Repository documentation map
documents.description: Navigation for repository documentation, current authority records, compatibility notes, and historical material.

documents.title: ADR-0001: Adopt an AI-native edge-kernel architecture
documents.description: Accepted for incremental migration on 2026-07-10. Runtime-composition clauses were amended by ADR-0003 on the same date; ADR-0003 governs where the two records differ.

documents.title: ADR-0002: Extract the SHM data plane and add a local durable outbox
documents.description: Accepted and implemented on 2026-07-10. Compatibility aggregate removal completed on 2026-07-13 after ADR-0014 rolling conformance passed.

documents.title: ADR-0003: Keep production services isolated over SHM and per-consumer events
documents.description: Accepted and implemented for the peripheral read paths on 2026-07-10.

documents.title: ADR-0004: Use capability-oriented canonical service names
documents.description: Accepted and implemented on 2026-07-10.

documents.title: ADR-0005: Make SHM the only core live-state plane
documents.description: Accepted and implemented on 2026-07-10.

documents.title: ADR-0006: Separate installation from IoT commissioning
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-11. The installation-context, fail-safe default, read-only setup plan, explicit plan apply, fresh-install refusal, health-check, and release-integrity clauses are implemented. In-place runtime upgrade and legacy…

documents.title: ADR-0007: Separate the Aether kernel from the AetherEMS distribution
documents.description: Accepted. Repository-local artifact and extraction gates were implemented on 2026-07-13. The physical AetherEMS repository split, downstream bootstrap CI, and EMS Console ownership are complete. AetherIot v0.5.0 supplied the signed…

documents.title: ADR-0008: Route external device actions through the application boundary
documents.description: Accepted and implemented for external device actions on 2026-07-11. The nonexistent instance-measurement compatibility surface and the rule engine's direct action-dispatch path were removed on 2026-07-12. Physical action-route mutations…

documents.title: ADR-0009: Introduce optional, industry-neutral data processing
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-11. Implementation notes reconciled on 2026-07-11.

documents.title: ADR-0010: Separate physical acquisition addresses from logical application points
documents.description: Implemented for all production and test graphs on 2026-07-13. The legacy aggregate was removed after the ADR-0014 compatibility matrix and rolling conformance contracts passed.

documents.title: ADR-0011: Govern channel desired state and runtime projection
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-12. The application, default SQLite/runtime adapter, and HTTP/CLI/MCP CRUD, lifecycle, and runtime-reconciliation migrations are implemented as one staged change. Offline bulk configuration import remains an explicitly…

documents.title: ADR-0012: Make contracts and Agent Skills the application surface
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-13.

documents.title: ADR-0013: Release one SDK facade instead of workspace crates
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-14.

documents.title: ADR-0014: Coordinate point and health SHM topology publication
documents.description: Accepted and implemented on 2026-07-13. The ADR-0002 legacy SHM aggregate was removed after the compatibility matrix and rolling-version contracts passed.

documents.title: ADR-0015: Assign configuration authority and reconcile applied projections
documents.description: Accepted and implemented on 2026-07-13.

documents.title: ADR-0016: Telemetry egress and the observability boundary
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-14. The channel-connectivity egress, the bounded-cardinality telemetry contract, and W3C trace-context propagation are implemented, with telemetry off by default pending a broker policy that grants the topic.

documents.title: ADR-0017: Experimental CloudLink MQTT edge foundation
documents.description: Accepted for an experimental edge implementation on 2026-07-15 and amended the same day for initial repository coordination. ADR-0018 supersedes the co-authority language with one pinned public AetherContracts authority.

documents.title: ADR-0018: Pinned AetherContracts consumption
documents.description: Accepted on 2026-07-15. AetherContracts v0.1.0-alpha.3 is the sole public contract authority. AetherIot and AetherCloud consume it through byte-identical aether-contracts.lock.json files. The current claim is distribution-only; both…

documents.title: ADR-0019: Establish AetherIoT as the product family and rename the edge product to AetherEdge
documents.description: Status: Accepted on 2026-07-16.

documents.title: ADR-0020: Integrate Home Assistant through an Edge bridge
documents.description: Accepted staged decision for delegated topology, observations, governed actions, resynchronization, backpressure, and authority.

documents.title: ADR-0021: CLI and MCP consume the remote application boundary
documents.description: Accepted.

documents.title: AetherCloud
documents.description: AetherCloud is the AI-native, industry-neutral, multi-cloud IoT fusion and control plane for AetherEdge runtimes and cloud-side workloads. It is a separate product, not a hosted copy of the edge runtime.

documents.title: AetherContracts
documents.description: AetherContracts is the public, language-neutral interoperability authority for AetherEdge, AetherCloud, and independent implementations.

documents.title: AetherEdge
documents.description: AetherEdge is the open-source, industry-neutral Linux edge runtime, Kernel, CLI, and Rust SDK formerly published from the AetherIot repository name.

documents.title: AetherEdge
documents.description: Build, integrate, diagnose, or generate applications for the AetherEdge AI-native edge kernel. Use for AetherEdge onboarding, SDK compositions, device and topology clients, read-only operations UIs, MCP integration, Domain Packs, or governed IoT commands where live-state authority and physical-device safety must be preserved.
